libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: jakarta.xml.bind-api is API-only. If tests exercise JAXBContext discovery, use test-local JAXBContextFactory/service-provider stubs or simple API/helper classes rather than assuming a bundled JAXB implementation. Avoid relying on SecurityManager package-access behavior for JDK 25 compatibility.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #4422 [Automation] java run fails for jakarta.xml.bind:jakarta.xml.bind-api:4.1.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Full end-to-end marshalling/unmarshalling with JAXBContext.newInstance normally requires a JAXB runtime implementation such as Eclipse JAXB RI, but that is not necessary for meaningful reachability coverage of the API artifact itself.
Adding a runtime implementation could shift coverage toward the implementation dependency rather than the jakarta.xml.bind-api metadata under test.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No deterministic setup is required: the resolved artifact is an API jar with only jakarta.activation-api as a transitive dependency, and existing meaningful coverage can exercise JAXB API internals such as ContextFinder, ServiceLoaderUtil, message bundles, helpers, annotations, and DatatypeConverter without Docker, environment variables, or an external runtime.
